paper_title: A review of factors influencing learners' gain of English proficiency

entities.description: The level of competence or skill in using the English language.
The ability of the learner to take responsibility for his or her own learning process.
Internal drives and emotional desires influencing language learning success.
Specific actions or techniques used by learners to improve their language acquisition.
Factor encompassing teacher competence, pedagogical skills, and subject mastery.
An approach to language teaching that emphasizes interaction as both the means and the ultimate goal of study.
A teaching approach focusing on the use of authentic language and on asking students to do meaningful tasks.
A standardized questionnaire used to measure language learning strategy use.
A literature search method where researchers consult the reference lists of relevant articles to find more sources.
Statistical methods used to determine relationships between variables like autonomy and proficiency.
A freely accessible web search engine that indexes the full text or metadata of scholarly literature.
A website which provides access to a large bibliographic database of scientific and medical publications.
The Education Resources Information Center, an online library of education research and information.
A high positive correlation found between learners' proficiency and adoption of strategies in the study by Gharbavi and Mousavi.
Participant count in Park's (1995) study on SILL and TOEFL scores.
Participant count in Dafei's (2007) study on autonomy and proficiency.
The field of education focused on teaching English to speakers of other languages.
